name: EXPRESSWAY
address: 1450 SE REED MARKET RD, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Utility Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.considerations: Multiple Access Points; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that emergency responders can easily navigate the property could be achieved by keeping pedestrian walkways and vehicle parking areas clear of obstructions, which may facilitate efficient movement. Clear signage and marked pathways leading to the main building would assist responders in quickly reaching critical areas. If the property features multiple access points, clearly identifying and marking these could enhance entry and exit strategies, optimizing responder staging and operations. Conversely, when a single access point is used, maintaining unobstructed entry by managing vehicular and pedestrian traffic in this area may improve rapid entry and exit. Additionally, maintaining open lines of sight and well-defined routes around the landscaped and utility areas could support responder visibility and access, further promoting effective emergency response activities on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Fuel Break; Scattered Shrubs; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation could improve defensible space and reduce the potential for fire spread. Maintaining the vegetated perimeter while managing the density of shrubs within it can help in reducing fuel continuity. The existence of an open fuel break and open terrain should be preserved to serve as effective barriers against wildfire progression. Furthermore, regularly assessing and managing scattered shrubs throughout the property can aid in minimizing the buildup of combustible materials and promote a safer environment. Consistent management and pruning of vegetation near structures will support long-term wildfire resilience and help prepare the property for potential fire incidents.
